truth.papers.A67.title: Low-Density Steels: Complex Metallurgy for Automotive Applications
truth.papers.A67.paper_scope: This review highlights the complex metallurgy of high-aluminum low-density steels, covering effects of Al on phase stability, thermodynamic modeling, microstructure evolution, and mechanical properties, and assesses their lightweighting potential for automotive applications.

prediction.papers.A67.title: Low-Density Steels: Complex Metallurgy for Automotive Applications
prediction.papers.A67.paper_scope: Overview of high-Al low-density steels covering thermodynamic CALPHAD modeling, microstructure evolution in ferritic, austenite-ferrite and austenitic/TRIPLEX grades, mechanical property relationships, and automotive lightweighting potential versus DP600.

truth.processes.proc_fe8al_recrystallization.d: Hot torsion tests at temperatures 900-1100°C and strain rates 0.1-10 s^-1 to study dynamic recrystallization mechanisms.
truth.processes.proc_2mn8al02c_cooling.descrip: Cooling from 1400°C at 10°C/s and quenching at 870°C, with isothermal holding at 870°C for 15 min.
truth.processes.proc_28mn94al092c_aging.descri: Solution treated at 1000°C for 10 min, water quenched, then aged at 500°C for 64 h.
truth.processes.proc_28mn94al092c_aging.proces: Aging time varied up to 64 h; final state used for structure properties.
truth.processes.proc_29mn96al056c_aging.descri: Solution treated at 850°C for 10 min, water quenched, then aged at 550°C for 1 h.
truth.processes.proc_74al82mn02c_sheet.descrip: Sheet forming and heat treatment to achieve microstructure and mechanical properties for crash performance evaluation.
truth.processes.proc_74al82mn02c_sheet.process: Exact processing route not detailed; properties as tested.
truth.processes.proc_93al258mn096c_sheet.descr: Sheet forming and heat treatment to achieve microstructure and mechanical properties for crash performance evaluation.
truth.processes.proc_93al258mn096c_sheet.proce: Exact processing route not detailed; properties as tested.

prediction.processes.proc_fe8al_recrystallization.d: Hot torsion testing in the temperature range 900–1100°C and strain-rate range 0.1–10 s⁻¹ to study dynamic recrystallization mechanisms.
prediction.processes.proc_8mn02c_01.description: Experimental alloy preparation and evaluation for density by picnometry and phase fractions via Thermo-Calc.
prediction.processes.proc_8mn02c_01.processes_notes: Applies to both 6% Al and 8% Al variants of the 8Mn-0.2C base.
prediction.processes.proc_2mn8al02c_cooling.descrip: Controlled cooling from the two-phase domain and isothermal treatment to study kappa-phase precipitation morphologies.
prediction.processes.proc_2mn8al02c_cooling.process: Cooling from 1400°C at 10°C/s with quenching at 870°C; isothermal hold at 870°C for 15 min.
prediction.processes.proc_28mn94al092c_aging.descri: Solution treatment followed by long-term aging to study intragranular kappa-phase precipitation in austenite.
prediction.processes.proc_28mn94al092c_aging.proces: Solution treated at 1000°C for 10 min and water quenched; aged at 500°C for times from 2 min to 64 h.
prediction.processes.proc_288mn94al092c_02.descript: Thermal mechanical processing to produce a range of austenitic grain sizes for Hall–Petch evaluation.
prediction.processes.proc_288mn94al092c_02.processe: Grain sizes from 1.65 μm to 40 μm obtained via varied processing parameters.
prediction.processes.proc_29mn96al056c_aging.descri: Solution treatment and aging to study ferrite ordering evolution.
prediction.processes.proc_29mn96al056c_aging.proces: Treated at 850°C for 10 min, water quenched, then aged at 550°C for 1 h.
prediction.processes.proc_85mn6alxsi02c_01.descript: Short-time annealing at 800°C to study partially recrystallized two-phase microstructures.
prediction.processes.proc_85mn6alxsi02c_02.descript: Short-time annealing at 1000°C to study recrystallized two-phase microstructures.
prediction.processes.proc_74al82mn02c_sheet.descrip: Processing to produce austenite-ferrite sheet for tensile testing and automotive benchmarking.
prediction.processes.proc_74al82mn02c_sheet.process: Exact continuous annealing parameters not detailed in text.
prediction.processes.proc_93al258mn096c_sheet.descr: Processing to produce fully austenitic sheet for tensile testing and automotive benchmarking.
prediction.processes.proc_93al258mn096c_sheet.proce: Exact thermomechanical processing parameters not detailed in text.
prediction.processes.proc_dp600_01.description: Reference industrial processing for DP600 automotive sheet.

prediction.processing_steps.sample_fe8al_recrystall: Strain rate range 0.1–10 s⁻¹. Discontinuous dynamic recrystallization (DDRX) at high T/high strain rate; continuous dynamic recrystallization (CDRX) at low T/low strain rate.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_8mn02c_6al_01.pr: Density evaluated by picnometry; phase fractions calculated with Thermo-Calc.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_8mn02c_8al_01.pr: Density evaluated by picnometry; phase fractions calculated with Thermo-Calc.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_2mn8al02c_cooled: Isothermal hold in ferrite+kappa phase domain leads to complete austenite decomposition into cocoon-like kappa structures.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_28mn94al092c_age: Aging times studied: 2 min, 16 min, 4 h, and 64 h.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_288mn94al092c_02: Various unspecified treatments to obtain austenitic grain sizes ranging from 1.65 μm to 40 μm.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_85mn6alxsi02c_01: Ferrite recrystallization incomplete at this temperature; pancake-shaped ferrite grains observed.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_85mn6alxsi02c_02: Higher annealing temperature yields more recrystallized ferrite microstructure.

truth.structures.sample_fe8al_recrystallized.o: Recrystallized ferritic microstructure; two dynamic recrystallization mechanisms observed: DDRX at high T/high strain rate, CDRX at lower T/low strain rate.
truth.structures.sample_2mn8al02c_cooled.overa: Eutectoid-like mixture of ferrite and kappa-phase, with complex 3D morphologies of kappa-phase growing along austenite/ferrite interfaces. Austenite largely decomposed.
truth.structures.sample_2mn8al02c_cooled.micro: non-lamellar eutectoid, complex 3D shapes, grows along austenite long axis
truth.structures.sample_28mn94al092c_aged.over: Austenitic matrix with intragranular nanosized kappa-phase precipitates (after 64 h aging). Some small amount of ferrite may be present (TRIPLEX concept).
truth.structures.sample_28mn94al092c_aged.micr: intragranular, aligned along <100> directions of austenite
truth.structures.sample_29mn96al056c_aged.over: Ferrite matrix with ordered structures: initially a mix of B2 and DO3, fully DO3 after aging. Ordering due to high Al content and partitioning.

prediction.structures.sample_2mn8al02c_cooled.micro: nonlamellar eutectoid precipitate at austenite/ferrite interface
at austenite/ferrite interface; grows along austenite long axis in 3D
cocoon-like structure after complete austenite decomposition
prediction.structures.sample_28mn94al092c_aged.over: Austenitic matrix with intragranular kappa precipitates and minor ferrite
prediction.structures.sample_28mn94al092c_aged.micr: intragranular in austenite, diffuse in initial state
intragranular in austenite, arranged along h100i directions after 64 h aging
prediction.structures.sample_29mn96al056c_aged.micr: bcc with B2 and DO3 ordering
prediction.structures.sample_85mn6alxsi02c_01.overa: Two-phase austenite-ferrite with partially recrystallized ferrite

truth.interfaces.sample_2mn8al02c_cooled.phase: Kappa-phase precipitates at austenite/ferrite interfaces during cooling, leading to eutectoid-like decomposition.

prediction.interfaces.sample_2mn8al02c_cooled.phase: Kappa phase precipitates at austenite/ferrite interfaces during slow cooling, forming a nonlamellar eutectoid morphology; in 3D kappa grows along the long axis of austenite. Isothermal treatment produces a cocoon-like kappa structure.
prediction.interfaces.sample_85mn6alxsi02c_01.phase: Austenite/ferrite phase distribution in two-phase low-density steel; ferrite recrystallization state changes significantly between 800°C and 1000°C anneals.

prediction.properties.sample_288mn94al092c_02.mecha: Engineering stress–strain curves tabulated for grain sizes 1.65–40 μm; see unmapped_findings for tabulated values.

prediction.performance.sample_74al82mn02c_sheet.ser: A-Pillar (anti-intrusion) in EuroNCAP 40% overlap front crash simulation
prediction.performance.sample_93al258mn096c_sheet.s: A-Pillar (anti-intrusion) in EuroNCAP 40% overlap front crash simulation

truth.characterization_methods.sample_2mn8al02: 3D observation of kappa-phase after deep etching
Kappa-phase growth along austenite long axis.
truth.characterization_methods.idx_2.purpose: Phase mapping and recrystallization study of 8.5Mn-6Al-XSi-0.2C alloy annealed at 800 and 1000°C
truth.characterization_methods.idx_2.key_findi: At 800°C, incomplete recrystallization, pancake ferrite grains; at 1000°C, equiaxed grains. Austenite indexing problematic at 800°C due to dislocations.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_28mn94al: Observe kappa-phase precipitation and ordering during aging
Kappa-phase precipitates cuboidal, 5-10 nm after 64 h; superlattice reflections in diffraction patterns.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_29mn96al: Extra spots indicate B2 and DO3 ordering; after 1 h aging, fully DO3 structure.

prediction.characterization_methods.sample_28mn94al: Observe intragranular kappa-phase precipitation evolution during aging at 500°C
Diffuse superlattice reflections in as-quenched state; after 4 h kappa phases are round and <5 nm; after 64 h they are cuboidal 5–10 nm and aligned along h100i directions of austenite.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_29mn96al: Study ferrite ordering evolution during aging at 550°C
As-quenched ferrite shows mixed B2 and DO3 ordering evidenced by {111} and {200} extra spots; after 1 h at 550°C ferrite becomes fully DO3 ordered with equal intensity of {111} and {200} reflections.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_85mn6alx: Map phase distribution and ferrite grain orientation after 800°C anneal
Ferrite grains are pancake-shaped and not fully recrystallized; austenite is heavily dislocated and difficult to index; local misorientations are present.
Map phase distribution and ferrite grain orientation after 1000°C anneal
Ferrite is more fully recrystallized compared to 800°C; grain morphology changes significantly with temperature.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_2mn8al02: Reveal 3D morphology of kappa-phase precipitation after deep etching
Kappa phase grows along the long axis of austenite; deep etching reveals complex 3D nonlamellar eutectoid morphology and cocoon structures after isothermal treatment.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_fe8al_re: Determine dynamic recrystallization mechanisms as a function of temperature and strain rate
DDRX activated at high temperature (900–1100°C) and high strain rate (0.5–10 s⁻¹); CDRX occurs at lower temperature/strain rate.
prediction.characterization_methods.idx_6.purpose: Calculate phase equilibria, isoplethal sections, and austenite phase fractions for Fe-Mn-Al-C system
prediction.characterization_methods.idx_6.key_findi: Latest Lindahl and Selleby database best reproduces fcc+bcc+kappa three-phase domain; deviation at high Al due to unmodeled kappa phase in some calculations.
prediction.characterization_methods.idx_7.purpose: Evaluate automotive weight-saving potential in EuroNCAP 40% overlap front crash
prediction.characterization_methods.idx_7.key_findi: Austenite-ferrite alloy achieves ~21% weight saving versus DP600; fully austenitic alloy achieves ~32–33% weight saving; thicknesses adapted to match energy absorption and section forces.

truth.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: Density calculations compared with experimental picnometry for 0.2C-8.5Mn alloys, good agreement at low Al.
truth.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: Predict density from phase fractions at room temperature

prediction.computational_details.idx_0.potential_mo: TCFE7 Steels/Fe-alloys database version 7 with Hallstedt/Lindahl-Selleby assessments
prediction.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: Density and austenite fraction compared with picnometry and experimental alloys; excellent agreement at low Al, deviation at high Al attributed to unmodeled kappa phase
prediction.computational_details.idx_1.validation_a: Material models based on experimental tensile data of the low-density alloys
prediction.computational_details.sample_288mn94al09: Experimental tensile data on 28.8Mn-9.4Al-0.92C alloy with grain sizes 1.65–40 μm
relating austenite grain size to yield strength and tensile strength
k_y = 461 MPa·μm^1/2 for yield strength; k_UTS = 351 MPa·μm^1/2 for ultimate tensile strength

truth.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Fig. 1: Density as a function of aluminum content for binary Fe-Al and quaternary 0.2C-8.5Mn alloys. Density decreases linearly with Al content; experimental data for quaternary alloys slightly lower than binary. Thermo-Calc calculation matched well at low Al, deviation at high Al possibly due to kappa-phase not considered.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Hall-Petch relation for 28.8Mn-9.4Al-0.92C alloy: yield strength coefficient 461 MPa·μm^{1/2}, tensile strength coefficient 351 MPa·μm^{1/2}.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Fig. 10: Influence of annealing temperature and time on tensile strength of 8.5Mn-0.2C based alloys; strength decreases with increasing temperature/time, with a relatively small change in MPa/°C.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: Fig. 12: Engineering stress-strain curves for fully austenitic 0.92C-28.8Mn-9.4Al alloy with grain sizes from 1.65 to 40 μm. Table data extracted from figure.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: Dynamic recrystallization map (Fig. 6) for Fe-8Al ferritic steel: DDRX activated at high temperature and high strain rate, CDRX at lower temperature and lower strain rate.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: Roping defects in ferritic Fe-Al alloys attributed to inhomogeneous grain orientations; DDRX preferred to limit roping.

prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Density reduction of 8–10% or higher is achieved versus low-carbon steels by aluminum additions; binary Fe-Al density decreases from ~7.25 g/cm³ at 6% Al to ~6.55 g/cm³ at 12.5% Al.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Quaternary 0.2C-8.5Mn (wt.%) alloys studied in this work show slightly lower density than binary Fe-Al by picnometry; continuous line in Fig. 1 corresponds to Thermo-Calc density calculation.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Dynamic recrystallization map for Fe-8% Al ferritic steel (Fig. 6): DDRX is activated at strain rates ≥0.5 s⁻¹ for 900°C, ≥0.5 s⁻¹ for 1000°C, and ≥0.1 s⁻¹ for 1100°C; below these thresholds DDRX is not activated.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: Engineering stress–strain curve data for 28.8Mn-9.4Al-0.92C at grain sizes 1.65–40 μm (Fig. 12 table): stress values range from ~400 MPa to ~990 MPa at elongations up to ~70%; exact tabulated values are present in the text figure data.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: Carbon content effect on yield strength in austenitic grades: decreasing C leads to a significant strength decrease, quantified as lower than 300 MPa per wt.% C but certainly higher than 187 MPa per wt.% C.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: Ferrite fraction effect on mechanical properties in austenitic grades: 2–5% ferrite increases yield strength by 30–50 MPa; above 30% ferrite yield strength decreases; ultimate tensile strength decreases by ~80 MPa when adding up to 39% ferrite; total elongation decreases 6% with only 3% ferrite and up to 16% with 39% ferrite.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: Annealing temperature and time decrease tensile strength of 8.5Mn-0.2C austenite-ferrite alloys continuously except at 800°C where incomplete ferrite recrystallization causes deviation; Al and Si act as solid-solution strengtheners.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: Aluminum greatly increases kinematic hardening in these steels according to recent studies.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_8.value: Automotive front side member (a) simulation results: DP600 reference mass 5.88 kg; austenite-ferrite (7.4Al-8.2Mn-0.2C) mass 4.63 kg (–1.25 kg, –21%); austenitic (9.3Al-25.8Mn-0.96C) mass 3.99 kg (–1.89 kg, –32%).
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_9.value: Automotive A-Pillar (b) simulation results: DP600 reference mass 6.11 kg; austenite-ferrite mass 4.84 kg (–1.27 kg, –21%); austenitic mass 4.12 kg (–1.99 kg, –33%).
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_10.value: Cost estimations (€/kg saved) of austenite-ferrite concept are similar to other less alloyed concepts despite higher alloying additions.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_11.value: Thermodynamic modeling history and database versions: Hallstedt database incorporating Chin et al., Umino et al., and Lindahl-Selleby assessments are compared; the Al-Mn-C system has not yet been evaluated and the kappa-phase model needs improvement.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_12.value: Isothermal section at 850°C of 8 wt.% Al FeMnAlC alloy (Fig. 4) shows three families: bcc (ferritic), bcc+fcc (two-phase), and fcc (austenitic/TRIPLEX) depending on C and Mn contents.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_13.value: During aging of 28.8Mn-9.4Al-0.92C, uniform and total elongation increase with increasing austenite grain size, but saturation occurs above ~10 μm.
